commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=641838cf29a85934de6aa1a8fe42208ba6456460)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Slack channel-name context, lazy Slack `conversations.info` name resolution, and tests so read-like Slack actions can authorize channel-ID targets against name-keyed allowlists.

PR surface: Source +96, Tests +105. Total +201 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main rejects channel-ID reads against name-keyed Slack allowlists, and PR head still has a source-reproducible wildcard fallback edge before lazy name lookup.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This external PR needs contributor real behavior proof and a small authorization-ordering update; do not queue an automated repair marker while the contributor proof gate is missing.

target.title: fix(slack): allow channel-id reads for name-allowlisted channels
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Slack action tools can target a channel by Slack channel ID, for example when reading a permalink or operating on a message outside the current thread. If an operator configured Slack channel access by human-readable channel name (for example `#allowed-channel`) with `dangerouslyAllowNameMatching` enabled, those channel-ID reads were rejected before the name allowlist could be applied.

That blocked legitimate read-like Slack actions such as reading messages, downloading files, listing pins, and listing reactions for channels that were configured as allowed by name.

## How This Changes Behavior
- Carries the current Slack channel name through threading tool context when the inbound transport provides it.
- Uses the current channel name only when the target channel matches the current context, so one allowed context cannot authorize a different target channel.
- Lazily resolves Slack conversation names through `conversations.info` when an action has only a channel ID and the account explicitly enabled name matching.
- Re-checks read authorization with the resolved channel name and still fails closed if no allowed name is found.
- Reuses the existing conversation info cache and returns names from that helper.

## Evidence
- `pnpm test:extension slack` — 101 files / 1333 tests passed.
- `pnpm vitest run extensions/slack/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/slack/src/channel-type.test.ts` — 2 files / 73 tests passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions` — passed.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` — passed.
- Added synthetic-fixture coverage for:
  - current-context channel-name authorization of a matching channel ID;
  - rejecting a different target channel even when the current context has an allowed name;
  - resolving a channel name via `conversations.info` before failing an allowlist read;
  - returning conversation names from the Slack channel-type helper.

## Privacy / Fixture Notes
All examples in this PR use fabricated Slack fixtures such as `#allowed-channel`, `C0123456789`, and `C9876543210`; no private workspace channel names, channel IDs, user IDs, or message timestamps are included.
target.author: jontsai
